title: Trends in the US data centre market
gist: A video-page listing of Benchmark analyst interviews and conference sessions around US data centre growth, reindustrialisation and energy storage: covering land/power/permitting constraints on US manufacturing, LG Energy Solution's shift to LFP for utility-scale storage, a US critical minerals stockpile framed as a price-stabilization tool, and energy storage described as both the backbone and bottleneck of the US grid transition.

insights.claim: US reindustrialisation is constrained not only by federal policy but by the local availability of land, power and permitting, which directly shapes the feasibility of critical minerals and manufacturing projects, per Savills' Ken Biberaj in an interview with Benchmark's Bryan Bille.
insights.speaker: Shan Tomouk, BESS & Energy Lead, Benchmark; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark; Matthew Bird,
insights.so_what: Investors evaluating US critical minerals or manufacturing projects should underwrite site-level land, power and permitting risk, not just federal subsidy exposure.
insights.quote: US reindustrialisation depends not just on policy, but on the availability of land, power and permitting.
insights.locator: Real estate and US reindustrialisation: Interview with Savills’ Ken Biberaj

insights.claim: LG Energy Solution is shifting production capacity toward LFP chemistry to serve accelerating US utility-scale energy storage demand, and says further domestic investment requires greater policy certainty, per an interview with LG's Bob Lee.
insights.speaker: Shan Tomouk, BESS & Energy Lead, Benchmark; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark; Matthew Bird,
insights.so_what: A top-tier cell maker reallocating capacity to LFP for utility storage signals growing US demand for LFP cathode material beyond EV applications, relevant for LFP precursor and cathode suppliers.
insights.quote: shifting capacity to LFP, serving utility-scale storage and the policy certainty needed to unlock further domestic investment.
insights.locator: US energy storage policy and manufacturing: Interview with LG Energy Solution’s Bob Lee

themes.theme: Critical minerals stockpile and defense supply chains

insights.claim: The US critical minerals stockpile is being assessed for its role in stabilizing volatile Western critical minerals prices, including a potential government role in price exchange mechanisms.
insights.speaker: Shan Tomouk, BESS & Energy Lead, Benchmark; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark; Matthew Bird,
insights.so_what: A government-backed price-stabilization or exchange mechanism for critical minerals would reduce price volatility risk for Western battery and defense supply chains and could set an effective price floor relevant to miners' project economics.
insights.quote: Assessing the US critical minerals stockpile and its role in stabilizing volatile Western prices, & the role of the government in price exchanges
insights.locator: Critical minerals in Defense Tech: The Hidden Backbone of Military Power

insights.claim: US energy storage is becoming both the backbone and the bottleneck of the US energy transition, as demand grows exponentially while supply chain sovereignty concerns persist.
insights.speaker: Shan Tomouk, BESS & Energy Lead, Benchmark; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark; Matthew Bird,
insights.so_what: A bottlenecked domestic storage supply chain implies pricing power and policy tailwinds for non-China storage cell and component manufacturers serving the US grid.
insights.quote: From exponential demand to supply chain sovereignty, energy storage is becoming the backbone—and bottleneck—of the U.S. energy transition.
insights.locator: Panel: Powering the Surge: Energy Storage at the Center of America’s Grid Transformation

insights.claim: SEIA and Benchmark jointly compiled proprietary grid and behind-the-meter data on US energy storage deployment, framed around stabilising the grid amid AI and data centre power demand growth.
insights.speaker: Shan Tomouk, BESS & Energy Lead, Benchmark; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark; Matthew Bird,
insights.so_what: A proprietary SEIA-Benchmark dataset on behind-the-meter storage could be a useful leading indicator of storage capex tied to AI data centre buildout, worth tracking in Benchmark's fuller report.
insights.quote: Presenting the report findings from SEIA & Benchmark’s proprietary grid and behind the meter data on US energy storage deployment
insights.locator: Keynote Data Presentation: ESS - Stabilising the grid in the age of AI and Data Centers

source_meta.author: Shan Tomouk, BESS & Energy Lead, Benchmark; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark; Matthew Bird,
